behaviors.description: Products marked as out-of-stock are excluded from the recommendation results regardless of their relevance score.
behaviors.justification: This is a pipeline handoff check (inventory status + ranking output). Unit tests on filter logic alone can miss wiring/order bugs where ranked items bypass availability filtering. No real OS/network/subprocess or AI-model judgment is required, so Integration is sufficient and System/Agentic are disqualified by behavior text.
behaviors.test_description: Run the recommendation service with a seeded catalog containing high-score in-stock and out-of-stock items; assert final returned list contains no out-of-stock product IDs.

behaviors.description: The user preference filter correctly excludes product categories the user has explicitly blocked in their settings.
behaviors.justification: Failure appears at module boundaries (user settings retrieval + category filtering + recommendation assembly). Unit tests on filter predicates alone can miss settings-handoff defects. No explicit real external contract or AI output dependence in the behavior text.
behaviors.test_description: Seed user settings with blocked categories and a mixed candidate set; execute recommendation generation and assert no returned item belongs to blocked categories.

behaviors.description: The recommendation algorithm ranks products that match the user's recent browsing patterns higher than products with no browsing signal.
behaviors.justification: This checks weighting behavior after browsing-signal ingestion, which is typically multi-module (history features + scorer + sorter). Unit tests of scoring math may miss feature-plumbing issues. Higher levels are disqualified because behavior text does not require a real ML/LLM service or external infra.
behaviors.test_description: Create products where only a subset matches recent browse signals; run ranking end-to-end in-process and assert matched products are ordered above no-signal controls under fixed inputs.

behaviors.description: Pagination returns the correct page of results, with stable ordering — requesting page 2 twice returns identical results if the underlying data hasn't changed.
behaviors.justification: The risk is interaction between sort stability and pagination slicing across modules; unit tests on paginator alone can miss upstream nondeterministic ordering. No explicit real HTTP/OS requirement and no AI dependency.
behaviors.test_description: Generate a fixed recommendation set larger than one page; request page 2 twice without data mutation and assert identical item IDs/order and correct offset/limit semantics.

behaviors.description: The recommendation algorithm surfaces at least one product from a category the user hasn't browsed before (serendipity requirement), avoiding a filter bubble.
behaviors.justification: This is an algorithmic diversification rule that must hold after full ranking/constraint composition; unit-only checks can miss interactions with other filters. Agentic is disqualified because behavior text does not require real model judgment quality from an external AI system.
behaviors.test_description: Use a user profile with concentrated browsing categories and a catalog containing viable unseen categories; run recommendation generation and assert at least one top-N result comes from an unseen category.

behaviors.description: A new user with no purchase or browsing history receives recommendations based on overall popularity rather than an empty result set.
behaviors.justification: Cold-start fallback depends on orchestration between history lookup, fallback strategy, and popularity source. Unit tests on fallback function alone can miss integration defects (e.g., empty history incorrectly short-circuits). No explicit external-system or AI-output requirement.
behaviors.test_description: Create a user with no history and a known popularity ranking; call recommendation generation and assert non-empty output aligned with popularity ordering rules.

behaviors.description: Click tracking records the correct product ID, position in the list, and timestamp for each recommendation the user interacts with.
behaviors.justification: This is a write/read handoff across recommendation presentation and tracking persistence. Unit tests can miss schema-mapping or position-index wiring bugs. System is not required because behavior text does not explicitly require real external API contract validation; local DB/filesystem round-trip is enough at Integration.
behaviors.test_description: Emit recommendation impressions, simulate clicks, then read tracking records from the persistence layer and assert product_id, rank position, and timestamp values are correctly stored per click.

behaviors.description: The response time for generating recommendations stays under 200ms for a catalog of 100,000 products.
behaviors.justification: This is a performance SLO that depends on real runtime/OS behavior and end-to-end execution cost at scale; mocked or unit-only tests can hide true latency bottlenecks. Integration was rejected because the behavior explicitly sets a response-time contract that must be measured in a real process context.
behaviors.test_description: Run a system-level benchmark against a 100k-product dataset in a production-like runtime path; measure end-to-end recommendation latency and assert p95 (or specified metric) is <200ms.

behaviors.description: When a recommended product is purchased by the user, the recommendation algorithm deprioritizes it in subsequent requests.
behaviors.justification: This is cross-module state propagation (purchase event -> user state/features -> ranking). Unit tests on ranking alone miss event ingestion and state update handoff failures. No explicit requirement for real external API contracts or AI-model nondeterminism.
behaviors.test_description: Generate recommendations, record a purchase for one recommended product, regenerate recommendations, and assert that product's rank drops (or is excluded) according to policy.

behaviors.description: The recommendation results are deterministic for the same user state — requesting recommendations twice with no intervening user activity returns the same ranked list.
behaviors.justification: Determinism here is an end-to-end property across data fetch, scoring, tie-breaking, and pagination/sorting; unit tests on isolated sorter functions can miss nondeterminism introduced in wiring. Higher levels are disqualified: behavior text does not require real external infrastructure contracts or AI/ML output variability.
behaviors.test_description: Freeze user/profile/catalog state, call recommendation generation twice in the same environment, and assert identical ordered product IDs across both runs.
